project_overview.project_development_objective: To improve learning outcomes and nutrition services for girls and boys, and to strengthen service delivery and accountability, in all regions including areas affected by conflict, droughts and high levels of refugees
project_overview.total_financing: US$405 million (US$400 million IDA + US$5 million GFF)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 97 million Ethiopians and 800,000 refugees, with particular focus on 2.2 million people in 29 selected woredas

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Tigray, parts of Afar, Amhara, Oromia and Benishangul-Gumuz affected by conflict; drought-affected areas; high refugee-hosting areas

key_components.description: Support basic service delivery at woreda level through General Purpose Grant and Human Capital Specific Purpose Grant, including for refugees and host communities

key_components.component_name: Component 2: IPF - Multisector Interventions and Capacity Building
key_components.description: Improve human capital outcomes in conflict/drought/refugee-affected areas and strengthen system capacity through technical assistance
key_components.financing: US$135 million (US$130 million IDA + US$5 million GFF)

key_components.description: Zero-dollar component that can be activated for emergency response

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Finance (MOF) through Channel One Programs Coordination Directorate (COPCD)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Steering Committee chaired by State Minister of MOF; Technical Committees at federal, regional and woreda levels; coordination with MOE, MOH, MOA, MOWE, MOWSA, MOPD
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF component; government procurement systems for PforR component
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Semi-annual Joint Review and Implementation Support missions; administrative data through EMIS, DHIS2, UNISE; impact evaluation surveys in SPG woredas

identified_risks.risk_description: Political transition, ethnic conflicts, armed conflict in northern Ethiopia, governance challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Decentralized service delivery, equitable resource allocation, peace agreement implementation monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Ongoing conflict in Tigray, Afar, Amhara regions; violence in western Oromia and Benishangul-Gumuz
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Flexible implementation arrangements, regional/zonal level management in high-risk areas, security assessments

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak multisectoral coordination, limited woreda capacity for SPG management, staff turnover
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Technical assistance, capacity building, establishment of coordination committees, dedicated planning staff

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited procurement capacity, weak internal controls, cash management issues, high staff turnover
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strengthened audit arrangements, procurement training, PFM capacity building, simplified procedures

identified_risks.risk_description: High inflation, foreign exchange shortages, declining tax-to-GDP ratio, debt sustainability concerns
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Counterpart funding arrangements, macroeconomic monitoring, home-grown economic reform program

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - existing government GRM at woreda level, World Bank GRS available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Focus on girls' secondary education completion, gender-sensitive teacher training, women's participation in planning and budgeting, support for Women Development Groups

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Operation designed to work in conflict-affected areas with flexible implementation arrangements, focus on social cohesion building, peace-building through equitable service delivery
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Multiple concurrent crises including COVID-19, climate shocks, civil conflict, high inflation, new refugee influxes from Somalia and potential from Sudan
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risk assessments required, implementation may be managed at regional/zonal level in high-risk areas, staff safety protocols
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - supports 800,000 refugees and host communities, includes refugee integration into national education and health systems, addresses needs of 512,000 drought-induced IDPs
